etymology.description: 進(進) (jìn, "advance") conveys moving forward or ahead, suggesting motion or progression into something. 入(入) (rù, "enter") directly means to step into or become part of a space or realm, conveying entry.
etymology.interpretation: 進(進) (jìn, "advance") implies moving forward towards a destination. 入(入) (rù, "enter") suggests completing the motion of crossing a boundary into a new space. Combine these to express the idea of 'entering' as moving forward into a space or state.

parts.english: enter; join; get into; go in

examples.chinese: 我们进入公园(我們進入公園)。
请在进入房间前脱鞋(請在進入房間前脫鞋)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng zài jìnrù fángjiān qián tuōxié.
examples.english: Please remove your shoes before entering the room.

examples.chinese: 他进入大学后学习很勤奋(他進入大學後學習很勤奮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jìnrù dàxué hòu xuéxí hěn qínfèn.
examples.english: He studied diligently after entering the university.
